findings.problem: Continuity contradiction: Ada has a multi-turn on-page intervention in this chapter even though canonical availability in outline/continuity_sheet.json places her on-page in chapters 01, 04, 05, 08, 10, 15, and 18. This creates a canon-state mismatch.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the support beat starting at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:423 and ending at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:496 so the real-time de-escalation call is handled by a chapter-09-available character (for example Luis), or defer Ada's live call to chapter_10 while keeping only a brief text here. Preserve the sobriety check and leverage warning function.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:423 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:496, Ada is not carrying a multi-exchange live conversation unless her canon availability is explicitly updated elsewhere; FAIL if Ada still delivers the same extended real-time coaching while continuity_sheet availability remains unchanged.

findings.problem: Sanitization drift via summary replacing scene: at peak fallout pressure, the chapter moves into a generic comment chorus ("This is brave", "This is irresponsible", etc.) instead of staging one concrete social consequence with a named relationship or stakeholder. Tension diffuses into abstraction.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace most of the montage starting at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:509 and ending at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:524 with one specific incoming message/call from a named person with actual leverage (family, sponsor, landlord, counsel, or donor-facing operator) and show Marisol's immediate embodied response and decision in-scene.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:509 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:524, there is at least one named-source consequence (not anonymous feed noise) plus Marisol's immediate action/reaction tied to that consequence; FAIL if the section remains primarily a generic social-media comment list.

findings.problem: Contraction/register drift for Marisol: a high-contraction character repeatedly defaults to uncontracted imperatives ("Do not ...") in high-pressure speech. The pattern reads stilted and weakens spoken realism under stress.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework Marisol's imperative lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:79, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:127, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:181, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:453 toward contraction-default phrasing unless a specific emphatic beat requires one full form.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least 3 of the 4 cited Marisol imperatives are contracted (for example "don't") and any remaining full-form line is clearly motivated by emphasis/formality in adjacent text; FAIL if most cited lines remain uncontracted without explicit dramatic rationale.

findings.problem: Recurring prose tic: the repeated "She wanted to ..." construction appears more than twice at climactic stress points, flattening cadence and signaling autopilot repetition.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the impulse-rendering syntax at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:191, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:192, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:507 by converting at least two of the repeated desire clauses into concrete action, sensory perception, or free-indirect thought with different sentence architecture.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:191, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:192, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:507, no more than one sentence keeps the exact "She wanted to" opening and the emotional escalation remains legible; FAIL if the repeated opening still dominates both moments.

findings.problem: Checklist-like meta phrasing ("Legal nouns first, Marisol thought. Stress tell.") over-literalizes character calibration and briefly breaks diegetic immersion.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:355 to show Nora's stress pattern through observable diction and Marisol's embodied recognition without explicit analytic labeling (remove the direct label "Stress tell").
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised sentence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:355 preserves Nora's legal-language escalation while eliminating explicit diagnostic label language; FAIL if the narration still names the pattern as a meta tag.

summary: Chapter 09 lands its causal spine and carries consequence cleanly into chapter 10, but it fails craft/dialogue/prose due to a high-severity continuity mismatch, a high-severity summary-level sanitization retreat at the fallout peak, contraction-level dialogue stiffness in Marisol's pressure speech, and repeated sentence formulas that flatten line-level texture.
